highlights.title: SpaceX Sells $25 Billion of Bonds, Cuts Interest Costs
highlights.description: SpaceX sold $25 billion of investment-grade bonds on Tuesday, marking the final step to replace the costly debt that had helped finance Elon Musk’s acquisition of X, then known as Twitter, as well as the expensive loans and bonds issued by xAI to bridge its rapid cash drain. Bloomberg's Manuel Baigorri reports. (Source: Bloomberg)

highlights.title: ByteDance Seeks $20 Billion in Its Largest-Ever Global Loan
highlights.description: ByteDance Ltd., the developer of TikTok, is in preliminary talks with banks for a borrowing of about $20 billion, people familiar with the matter said, in what would be the firm’s largest offshore loan yet at a time when it’s boosting investments in artificial intelligence.

highlights.title: Musk’s SpaceX Adds Billions in Debt While Cutting Interest Costs
highlights.description: Elon Musk is no stranger to financial alchemy. His latest feat: adding billions of dollars of debt onto his cash-burning business empire while simultaneously cutting its annual interest burden.

highlights.title: YouTube settles early test case over social media harm to children
highlights.description: Google has settled a lawsuit with a minor known who claimed that social media platforms harmed them.

highlights.title: Leveraged Korea ETFs Sold Estimated $6 Billion of Shares in Rout
highlights.description: Leveraged exchange-traded funds tracking Samsung Electronics Co. or SK Hynix Inc. probably sold a combined $6 billion of the Korean chipmakers’ shares Tuesday to maintain their ratios, underscoring such products are amplifying market moves, Bloomberg Intelligence says.

highlights.title: SK Hynix Seeks $29 Billion With US Listing to Fund AI Boom
highlights.description: SK Hynix Inc. is planning to raise 45.45 trillion won ($29.4 billion) in a landmark US listing, as companies along the artificial intelligence supply chain rush to increase capacity to meet memory chip demand.

highlights.title: Morgan Stanley doubles China humanoid robot shipment forecast as commercialization accelerates
highlights.description: Morgan Stanley has sharply raised its outlook for China's humanoid robotics market, as early commercial deployment in real-world scenarios accelerated.

highlights.title: Alphabet added to Dow Jones Industrial Average, replacing Verizon
highlights.description: Alphabet will replace Verizon in the Dow Jones Industrial Average.

highlights.title: Data Center Buildout Limited by Labor Shortages, Saint-Gobain Says
highlights.description: Saint-Gobain CEO Benoit Bazin tells Bloomberg Television that labor bottlenecks are limiting data center build-out in North America, a development that’s coming to Europe. Saint-Gobain manufactures glass products and construction materials. (Source: Bloomberg)

highlights.title: Philippine Tycoon Boosts PhilWeb Investment in Online Gaming Push
highlights.description: Philippine tycoon Lance Gokongwei is acquiring a 15% stake in online gaming technology firm PhilWeb Corp., boosting the company’s foray into artificial intelligence and the billionaire’s exposure to the gaming industry.

business.title: SpaceX Sells $25 Billion of Bonds, Cuts Interest Costs
business.description: SpaceX sold $25 billion of investment-grade bonds on Tuesday, marking the final step to replace the costly debt that had helped finance Elon Musk’s acquisition of X, then known as Twitter, as well as the expensive loans and bonds issued by xAI to bridge its rapid cash drain. Bloomberg's Manuel Baigorri reports. (Source: Bloomberg)

business.title: OHB raises funding for expansion, acquisitions
business.description:
German space company OHB will raise about half a billion euros through a stock sale to allow the company to expand facilities and pursue potential acquisitions.
The post OHB raises funding for expansion, acquisitions appeared first on SpaceNews.

business.title: Interview: we meet with Port of Long Beach CEO Dr. Noel Hacegaba
business.description: On today’s authoritative episode of Quick Charge, we’re talking all about how ports and port authorities are cleaning up their acts with the man in charge of the busiest port in North America – Port of Long Beach CEO Dr. Noel Hacegaba.

ai.title: Philippine Tycoon Boosts PhilWeb Investment in Online Gaming Push
ai.description: Philippine tycoon Lance Gokongwei is acquiring a 15% stake in online gaming technology firm PhilWeb Corp., boosting the company’s foray into artificial intelligence and the billionaire’s exposure to the gaming industry.

ai.title: BlackRock-Backed AIP, KKR Said to Court Stack Asia Data Centers
ai.description: The Artificial Intelligence Infrastructure Partnership and Brookfield Asset Management Ltd. are among possible bidders for Stack Infrastructure Inc.’s data centers in the Asia-Pacific region, according to people familiar with the matter.

ai.title: HSBC Wealth Survey Shows AI Losing Out to Humans in Key Areas
ai.description: For the truly rich, artificial intelligence can’t compete with human advisers in handling many of their needs, according to a report from HSBC Holdings Plc.

ai.title: Tencent Testing New AI Agent for WeChat Workplace App
ai.description: Tencent Holdings Ltd. is preparing to launch an AI agent for its Slack-like enterprise communication app, intensifying a high-stakes battle among Chinese tech giants to lock users into their ecosystems in the post-ChatGPT era.

ai.title: Indian Tech’s Nifty Share Shrinks to Record Low on AI Worries
ai.description: India’s software exporters are steadily losing their sway on the country’s stock market as concerns over artificial intelligence-led disruption trigger a prolonged selloff in the sector.

ai.title: Someone got a Google Home Speaker early, and the big downside is a fixed power cable
ai.description: The Google Home Speaker is set to start shipping very soon but, ahead of that, one user was able to buy one from a retail store early. They’ve since shared their first impressions, which unfortunately confirm that the speaker has a fixed power cable that’s also pretty short.

ai.title: Meta pauses mandatory AI training program that tracked employee keystrokes after internal data leak exposed sensitive staff information company-wide — employees express frustration over poor handling of data
ai.description: Meta has paused an internal AI training program after employee conversations, keystrokes, transcripts, and performance-related data were reportedly exposed across the company.

ai.title: Masayoshi Son Calls AI Bubble Talk an ‘Insult,’ Delays Retiring
ai.description: SoftBank Group Corp.’s Masayoshi Son brushed aside concerns about an AI bubble, saying that such a characterization is inappropriate for a revolution that’s only just begun. He vowed to remain at the helm of his investment group much longer to capitalize on the AI boom.

ai.title: Germany went off the rails as wireless outage saw all trains cancelled
ai.description: Unexplained GSM-R failure at Deutsche Bahn caused confusion and delay

space.title: Musk’s SpaceX Adds Billions in Debt While Cutting Interest Costs
space.description: Elon Musk is no stranger to financial alchemy. His latest feat: adding billions of dollars of debt onto his cash-burning business empire while simultaneously cutting its annual interest burden.

chips_cloud.title: YouTube settles early test case over social media harm to children
chips_cloud.description: Google has settled a lawsuit with a minor known who claimed that social media platforms harmed them.

chips_cloud.title: UBS' Fowler Sees Value in US Cyclicals, Pharmaceuticals
chips_cloud.description: UBS Chief European Equity Strategist and Head of Derivates Gerry Fowler discusses the factors behind the tech selloff and where he sees value in markets. "You're going to get volatility — this isn't the first time we've seen this," Fowler tells Bloomberg Television. "It's an artifice of market structure that I suspect is not going away." (Source: Bloomberg)

chips_cloud.title: Chinese supercomputer using local processors heads TOP500 list
chips_cloud.description: Use of Arm cores and Linux mean Beijing hasn’t broken away from the world

robotics.title: Morgan Stanley doubles China humanoid robot shipment forecast as commercialization accelerates
robotics.description: Morgan Stanley has sharply raised its outlook for China's humanoid robotics market, as early commercial deployment in real-world scenarios accelerated.

crypto.title: Bitcoin drops toward $62,000 as the chip selloff deepens for a second day
crypto.description: A renewed rout in semiconductor stocks pulled risk assets lower again, and crypto kept sliding. Bitcoin is down 5% on the week, with ether and the memecoins falling harder.
